class.name: FmodAudioEffectSpectrumAnalyzer
class.brief_description: Performs real-time frequency spectrum analysis on audio signals for visualization and analysis.
class.description: FmodAudioEffectSpectrumAnalyzer analyzes the frequency content of audio passing through an audio bus in real-time using Fast Fourier Transform (FFT). This is essential for creating audio visualizations such as spectrum analyzers, equalizer displays, or reactive audio effects. The effect processes incoming audio and provides frequency magnitude data that can be queried using [method get_magnitude_for_frequency_range]. You can analyze specific frequency ranges (e.g., bass, midrange, treble) independently. The [member fft_size] determines the frequency resolution: larger FFT sizes provide finer frequency detail but require more processing and have worse time resolution. The [member buffer_length] affects how much audio history is kept for analysis.

method.name: get_magnitude_for_frequency_range
method.description: Returns the magnitude (amplitude) of the specified frequency range as a [Vector2]. The [code]x[/code] component contains the left channel magnitude and [code]y[/code] contains the right channel magnitude. The [param begin] and [param end] parameters specify the frequency range in Hz (e.g., 20.0 to 200.0 for bass frequencies). The [param mode] determines how the magnitude is calculated across the range: [constant MAGNITUDE_AVERAGE] calculates the average magnitude, while [constant MAGNITUDE_MAX] returns the peak magnitude. Magnitude values are in linear scale (0.0 to 1.0+). Use [code]linear_to_db()[/code] to convert to decibels for display purposes.

members.member: The internal buffer length in seconds used for spectrum analysis. A larger buffer provides more stable analysis results but increases latency. The default value of 2.0 seconds is suitable for most visualization applications.

The FFT (Fast Fourier Transform) size used for spectrum analysis. Larger FFT sizes provide better frequency resolution (more frequency bins) but worse time resolution and higher CPU usage. See [enum FFTSize] for available options. The default [constant FFT_SIZE_1024] provides a good balance for general visualization.

constants.constant: 256-point FFT. Lower frequency resolution but better time resolution. Useful for analyzing transient sounds or when low latency is required.
512-point FFT. Moderate frequency resolution suitable for simple visualizations.
1024-point FFT (default). Good balance between frequency and time resolution. Recommended for most spectrum analyzer visualizations.
2048-point FFT. Higher frequency resolution for detailed spectrum analysis. Best for visualizing complex audio or musical content.
4096-point FFT. Very high frequency resolution but poor time resolution. Best for analyzing low-frequency content or when precise frequency detection is needed.
Maximum FFT size option. Provides the highest frequency resolution available.
Calculates the average magnitude across the specified frequency range. This provides a smoother response that represents the overall energy in the range.
Returns the maximum (peak) magnitude within the specified frequency range. This provides a more responsive visualization that highlights the strongest frequencies in the range.
